source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/05_Blue (G1417MCW 외 7종)/▶ 시험소/06. Usability 보완_250103/05. 주요 부품 인증서 + 사양서/60-2230C/CS-RegulatoryInfo-ST60-2230C v1_4.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Laird Connectivity regulatory information for the ST60-2230C dual band WLAN plus Bluetooth adapter, including country certifications, antenna limits, labeling, and integrator obligations.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use as regulatory integration guidance for OEM host-device documentation, external labels, approved antennas, and regional compliance statements for ST60-2230C.
keyPoints: The ST60-2230C holds listed certifications for USA FCC, Canada ISED, Japan MIC, Taiwan NCC, and Korea KC.
OEMs may use antennas of like type and equal or lesser gain, except Japan requires antennas to be listed on the MIC certificate before use.
Host products must include required FCC, ISED, RED, Taiwan, and other regional regulatory statements and labels where applicable.
Australia/New Zealand RCM is identified as pending, and OEMs must verify final end-product compliance and obtain their own SDoC.
Version 1.4 dated 14 Mar 2024 removed an expired SRRC certificate.

entities.name: Dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter
entities.evidence: Product Description Dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter

entities.name: Brian Petted
entities.evidence: Name of Authorized Person: Brian Petted, Technology Leader

entities.name: Ryan Urness
entities.evidence: Name of Authorized Person: Ryan Urness, Director of Test Services

concepts.name: ST60-2230C country certifications
concepts.evidence: The ST60-2230C holds current certifications in the following countries:

concepts.name: Approved antenna types and gains
concepts.evidence: The antennas listed below were tested for use with the ST60-2230C.

concepts.name: OEM antenna substitution
concepts.evidence: The OEM can choose a different manufacturer’s antenna but must make sure it is of same type and that the gain is less than or equal to the antenna that is approved for use.

concepts.name: Japan MIC antenna certificate requirement
concepts.evidence: If your antenna is not on the approved list, regardless of whether it is comparative, it must be added to the certificate before it can be used in Japan.

concepts.name: FCC host labeling
concepts.evidence: The end product must be labeled in a visible area with the following: Contains FCC ID: SQG-60SIPT

concepts.name: ISED modular integration
concepts.evidence: The OEM integrator is still responsible for testing their end-product for any additional compliance requirements required with this module installed.

concepts.name: EU Radio Equipment Directive statements
concepts.evidence: The following Radio Equipment Directive (RED) statements must be added in their entirety and without modification into a prominent place in the user’s guide

concepts.name: UKCA declaration of conformity
concepts.evidence: We, Laird Connectivity, declare under our sole responsibility that the essential test suites have been carried out and that the above product to which this declaration relates is in conformity with all the applicable requirements outlined above

concepts.name: RCM end-product responsibility
concepts.pageTarget: Australia New Zealand RCM end-product certification
concepts.evidence: All end-products require their own certification (SDoc). You will not be able to leverage the module certification and ship product into the country.

evidence.claim: The document is Regulatory Information v1.4.
The document revision date for version 1.4 is 14 Mar 2024.
The product is ST60-2230C.
The product is described as a dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Description Dual band 2x2 802.11ac WLAN plus Bluetooth 5.1 dual mode adapter

evidence.claim: The ST60-2230C has FCC ID SQG-60SIPT.
The ST60-2230C has Canada ISED ID 3147A-602230C.
The ST60-2230C has Japan MIC ID 003-170090.
The ST60-2230C has Taiwan NCC certificate number CCAF23Y10200T7.
The ST60-2230C has Korea KC certificate number MSIP-CRM-LAI-60-2230C.
The 60-2230C has China CMIIT ID 2017DJ2473.
The OEM may choose another vendor antenna of like type and equal or lesser gain under FCC guidance.
evidence.evidenceText: The OEM is free to choose another vendor’s antenna of like type and equal or lesser gain as an antenna appearing in the table and still maintain compliance. Reference FCC Part 15.204(c)(4)

evidence.claim: Japan requires non-listed antennas to be added to the MIC certificate before use.
evidence.evidenceText: If your antenna is not on the approved list, regardless of whether it is comparative, it must be added to the certificate before it can be used in Japan.

evidence.claim: The FCC host product label must state Contains FCC ID: SQG-60SIPT.
evidence.evidenceText: The end product must be labeled in a visible area with the following: Contains FCC ID: SQG-60SIPT

evidence.claim: The FCC authorization is no longer valid if co-location or other stated conditions cannot be met.
evidence.evidenceText: If this condition cannot be met ... then the FCC authorization is no longer considered valid and the FCC ID cannot be used on the final product.

evidence.claim: The Canada portable RF exposure minimum separation distance for portable use is limited to 15 mm assuming a 2 dBi antenna.
evidence.evidenceText: The minimum separation distance for portable use is limited to 15mm assuming use of antenna with 2 dBi of gain.

evidence.claim: The Japanese host device is recommended to bear the GITEKI mark and certification number.
evidence.evidenceText: It is recommended that the host device bears a label showing the Japanese “GITEKI” mark and the certification number

evidence.claim: The Taiwan host product must display the RF module certificate number CCAF23Y10200T7.
evidence.evidenceText: You must place “本產品內含射頻模組： CCAF23Y10200T7” ... on the host product in such a location that it can be seen by an operator at the time of purchase.

evidence.claim: Australia/New Zealand RCM is pending and final end-products require their own SDoC.
evidence.evidenceText: RCM: Pending ... All end-products require their own certification (SDoc). You will not be able to leverage the module certification and ship product into the country.
evidence.pageTarget: Australia New Zealand RCM end-product certification

evidence.claim: The UK declaration of conformity was issued on March 28, 2023.
The EU declaration of conformity was issued in August 2020.
The EU declaration states conformity with Article 3 of Directive 2014/53/EU when used for intended purpose.
evidence.evidenceText: the above product to which this declaration relates is in conformity with all the applicable essential requirements of Article 3 of the EU Directive 2014/53/EU, when used for its intended purpose.

reviewFlags.reason: Source lists multiple dates: version 1.4 date 14 Mar 2024, UK declaration date March 28, 2023, EU declaration date August 2020, and copyright 2023.
Product model appears both as ST60-2230C and 60-2230C in the source text.
Canada section contains a table labeled 'Model MIC ID' while the surrounding text identifies ISED/IC 3147A-602230C.
Australia/New Zealand RCM status is stated as pending and requires end-product SDoC; module certification cannot be leveraged for shipment.
Revision history says version 1.4 removed an expired SRRC certificate while the source still contains a China approval statement and CMIIT ID.
